human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for retail-banking boundary discipline, source quality, dispute and funds-availability handling, suspicious-activity escalation posture, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to retail-banking service, support, dispute-status, funds-availability, complaint, and escalation work tied to account, transaction, and servicing records.

Authority sources cover CFPB Regulation E resources and rule text, Federal Reserve Regulation CC guidance, OCC retail-banking complaint resources, FFIEC suspicious-activity reporting guidance, FinCEN SAR-supporting-document constraints, Nacha ACH risk controls, and ISO 20022 artifact normalization.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing prerequisites, unsupported overrides, complaint repeat failures, suspicious-activity escalation, disclosure and reconciliation conflict, terminology fidelity, low-confidence escalation, public-source conflict, tenant adaptation, and adjacent-lane routing.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/financial-services/retail-banking-support-specialist/ so retail-banking support stays separate from fraud, AML, legal, underwriting, and servicing-operations lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around fee overrides, hold releases, suspicious-activity communication, and legal or underwriting requests.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated retail-banking support specialist that supports evidence-backed service handling and policy-aware escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
